# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Http\Response;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Validator;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Fetch and display a website preview.
     *
     * @param  \Illuminate\Http\Request  $request
     * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response
     */
    public function preview(Request $request)
    {
        // 1. Validate the incoming URL
        $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
            'url' => 'required|url',
        ]);

        if ($validator->fails()) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid URL provided.',
                'messages' => $validator->errors()
            ], 400);
        }

        $url = $request->input('url');

        // 2. Initialize cURL
        $ch = curl_init();

        // 3. Set cURL options
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, $url);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true); // Return the transfer as a string
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION, true); // Follow redirects
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS, 5); // Limit redirects to 5
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_TIMEOUT, 10); // Max 10 seconds for the entire operation
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT, 5); // Max 5 seconds to connect
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, true); // Verify SSL certificate
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST, 2); // Verify hostname against certificate
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_USERAGENT, 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36 LinkPreviewBot/1.0');
        // Optionally, set a maximum download size to prevent very large files
        // curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_BUFFERSIZE, 128 * 1024); // 128KB buffer, not a max size
        // curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE, 1 * 1024 * 1024); // Max 1MB file size, but this is for upload.
        // For download, you'd need a callback function for CURLOPT_WRITEFUNCTION to limit.
        // For simplicity, I'll omit explicit size limiting for now, but it's a good production consideration.

        // 4. Execute cURL
        $content = curl_exec($ch);

        // 5. Check for cURL errors
        if (curl_errno($ch)) {
            $error_msg = curl_error($ch);
            curl_close($ch);
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Failed to fetch URL content.',
                'curl_error' => $error_msg
            ], 500);
        }

        $http_code = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);

        if ($http_code >= 400) {
            curl_close($ch);
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Failed to fetch URL content. HTTP status code: ' . $http_code,
                'http_code' => $http_code
            ], $http_code);
        }

        // 6. Close cURL
        curl_close($ch);

        // 7. Return the content
        // Since it's a "preview" and the prompt says "displays website previews",
        // returning the raw HTML content is the direct interpretation.
        // For a true "link preview", one would parse this content to extract
        // title, description, images (Open Graph, Twitter Cards, etc.)
        // but the prompt is specific about fetching and displaying content.
        return new Response($content, 200, ['Content-Type' => 'text/html']);
    }
}
